title: Japan draws with Netherlands in World Cup debut
summary: The Japanese national team managed a 1-1 draw with a header goal from Ogawa, showing great resilience.

title: U.S. and Iran agree to end armed conflict
summary: President Trump and the Iranian government will reach an agreement to stop hostilities, mediated by Switzerland and with the approval of Gulf countries.

title: G7 summit in Évian addresses artificial intelligence and Iran crisis
summary: G7 leaders debate AI risks, the situation in the Middle East, and cooperation on critical minerals.

title: Former Giants manager will not face charges for assault on his daughter
summary: The Tokyo Public Prosecutor's Office decided not to prosecute Shinnosuke Abe, former manager of the Giants, for the alleged assault on his eldest daughter.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi meets with her Italian counterpart Meloni
summary: Both leaders agreed to strengthen cooperation in advanced technology and signed a joint declaration.

title: Nikkei index surpasses 69,000 points for the first time in its history
summary: The Japanese stock market closed higher driven by the peace agreement between the U.S. and Iran and expectations of economic growth.

title: Embezzlement of 180 million yen in the Red Feather charity campaign in Hokkaido
summary: An employee allegedly diverted funds from the donation campaign, and authorities are investigating possible misuse of resources.

title: 12-year-old boy drowns in a river in Saga prefecture
summary: A middle school student was found dead in a river, and authorities are investigating the circumstances of the accident.

title: Financial scandal rocks Hyogo's Ishin party over alleged overcharging
summary: The Ishin parliamentary group in Hyogo faces accusations of having received excess political funds, and it is considering requesting the return.

title: Myanmar's president visits China seeking economic support
summary: The leader of the Burmese military junta meets with President Xi Jinping to strengthen trade ties and gain political support.

title: Russia attacks Ukraine with missiles damaging a historic monastery and killing nine
summary: The Russian attack on Kyiv and other Ukrainian regions caused fatalities and severe damage to a UNESCO World Heritage monastery.

title: Mayor of Shimotsuma, Ibaraki, found dead in a drainage ditch
summary: The mayor's body was found in a drainage ditch, and the cause of his death is being investigated.

title: Iran-U.S. agreement alleviates fertilizer prices in Japan but inflation will persist
summary: Analysts point out that the reduction of tensions in the Middle East will benefit the Japanese economy, although the price relief may not be felt until next year.

title: Japan to launch H3 rocket number 9 in August with commercial satellites
summary: The mission will mark the resumption of commercial flights after the successful test of Japan's flagship rocket.

title: Alert for torrential rain and possible formation of linear precipitation bands in Okinawa
summary: Heavy rainfall is expected in the region, with risk of floods and landslides.

title: Man survives bear attack using repellent spray in Japan
summary: A man managed to repel a bear that attacked him while carrying a deterrent spray, remaining unharmed.

title: Chaos at the launch of a 60,000 yen luxury watch in Ginza, Tokyo
summary: Limited sale caused long lines and reselling, drawing criticism over event management.

title: British PM proposes banning social media for under-16s
summary: The measure seeks to protect children from online dangers, though it faces criticism over its implementation.

title: Japan announces its squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup
summary: The list of called-up players was unveiled ahead of the international tournament.

title: Son of Norway's crown princess sentenced to four years in prison
summary: The 27-year-old was sentenced for assault and other crimes, in a case that affects the royal family's popularity.
